title: SSH Host Runtime Inventory
objective: Use the restored private-mesh SSH path over `olares-mesh` or direct `olares@100.64.0.1` to capture the still-missing read-only Olares host runtime inventory, installed-app status, private-lane timer posture, and host repo-clone evidence without reopening generic Olares implementation or mutating host runtime.
dependency_note: Packet 004 restored the trusted private-mesh path and proved non-interactive SSH to `olares@100.64.0.1` and the explicit `olares-mesh` alias. The next truthful move is no longer browser-terminal fallback. It is the still-missing host runtime inventory over the restored SSH path.
required_inputs: The Packet 004 closure proving `BackendState: Running`, workstation mesh IP `100.64.0.2`, peer `olares` online at `100.64.0.1`, and successful non-interactive SSH over the TermiPass interface
The Step 3 decision-surface verdict that TASK-021, TASK-023, and TASK-025 remain open largely because host runtime and host clone truth were not directly inspected
The existing trusted host fingerprint in docs/architecture/OLARES-SSH-HOSTKEY-RECONCILIATION-2026-05-01.md
Current workstation SSH configuration containing the explicit `olares-mesh` alias or a direct `olares@100.64.0.1` path
constrained_outputs: A dated handoff recording read-only host runtime inventory for Docker, K3s or Helm, installed apps, ports, volumes, networks, and relevant private-lane timers or units
A read-only statement of current host repo-clone path, branch, commit, cleanliness, and any obvious drift against the canonical workstation boundary if that evidence is inspectable without mutation
A refreshed statement of whether Packet 001's missing host-runtime inventory is now satisfied
A refreshed statement of whether VS Code Remote-SSH is technically viable through the explicit mesh alias based on actual SSH evidence rather than only local probes
No installs, no promotions, no ingress changes, no auth changes, no service restarts, no Helm changes, no Kubernetes mutation, no runtime mutation, and no canonical-hosting changes
validation_steps: Use the explicit mesh SSH path (`olares-mesh` preferred, direct `olares@100.64.0.1` acceptable) and confirm the host fingerprint matches the trusted fingerprint already recorded.
Capture read-only identity and environment evidence first: hostname, user, date, kernel, tool presence, and repo-clone paths.
Capture read-only runtime inventory for Docker, K3s or Helm, installed routes or apps, ports, volumes, networks, and relevant namespaces or pods if those surfaces are present.
Capture read-only evidence for installed `forms-engine` and `p6-ingest` host state if inspectable.
Capture read-only evidence for the private-lane backup and restore-drill timer surfaces if inspectable.
Capture host repo-clone path, branch, commit, and cleanliness if that evidence is visible without performing any git mutation.
Stop without widening scope if SSH degrades, host trust mismatches, or the session begins requiring mutation, elevation beyond the bounded read-only commands, or auth-repair work.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes read-only SSH-based host inspection only. It does not authorize database writes, host mutation, service changes, ingress changes, auth changes, or code-hosting changes.
closure_note: Not yet executed. This packet exists because Packet 004 restored the trusted mesh SSH path and made the missing host runtime inventory the smallest truthful next move.
next_action: Execute this packet through the explicit mesh SSH path while the restored TermiPass and Headscale state remains healthy. Retain Packet 004B only as a fallback if the mesh SSH path regresses and authenticated browser-terminal access remains available.
